content.full_text:
This is a letter from the State of California Fair Political Practices Commission dated September 25, 1984. The letter is addressed to William L. Gates at Catalano & Hawkins, Lloyds Bank Building, One Almaden Boulevard, Suite 600, San Jose, CA 95113-2265.

The letter states:

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our Advice No. A-84-233

Dear Mr. Gates:

This is to confirm my oral advice to you that Senator Alquist may conduct fundraising events at which he provides contributors with tickets to the Forty-Niners football games, dinners, tail-gate parties, and memorabilia, without creating any special problems or reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act, so long as all expenses are paid by, and all proceeds are paid to, his campaign committee and are reported in the usual manner.

If you have any further questions, please feel free to contact us again.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Milman
General Counsel
